cycle_id: F23 resolution — atlas DSL v2 Layer 4 vacuous-PASS sealed
trigger: F23 falsifier emitted VACUOUS_PASS / HIT in DSL v2 ω-cycle review
scope: tool/atlas_dsl_v2_regression.sh Layer 4 — fix vacuous PASS where serializer silently emits empty stdout for atlas main subset, both shasums collapse to sha256(empty)=e3b0c44298fc1c14… and byte-eq trivially holds
abstraction: raw 23 schema-guard surface — silent failure must not collapse into vacuous identity
raw 70 multi-axis-verify — Layer 4 stress axis was orthogonal to Layers 1-3 only by intent, not by execution
raw 71 falsifier-retire-rule — F23 stays as CLEAN guard, not retired (regression detection on future serializer changes)
raw 73 structural-admissibility — chosen OPT preserves serializer schema; surfaces drift via fix to caller, not parser semantics

axes.name: investigation — type alphabet hypothesis falsified
axes.finding: atlas main uses @P @C @F @L @R @S @X @E — ALL already in serializer's allowed set (P/C/F/L/R/S/X/M/T/E per _type_to_category lines 160-174). Type alphabet is NOT the gap.

axes.name: investigation — real failure surfaced via direct serializer call
axes.method: manual subset 100 entries → run serializer → stderr emitted '__ATLAS_DSL_V2_SERIALIZER__ FAIL schema-guard read-n6 fails=0 bad_grades=2  bad-grade: meta_fp:10*!:invalid char in token `!`  bad-grade: persistence_threshold:10*!:invalid char in token `!`'
axes.finding: serializer rejects atlas main grades. parse_compound_grade (line 81) only accepts ONE marker (* | ! | ?) at line 98; atlas main uses combined [10*!] (verified+breakthrough). Plus alien-index notation [A]/[B]/[XY]/[K3]/[N!] and plain numeric [24128]/[642]/[743]/[01].
axes.grade_inventory_atlas_main: 52 distinct grade tokens; v2-incompatible families: [10*!] dual-marker; [A]/[B]/.../[XY]/[K3] alien-index alphabet; [24128]/[642]/[743] numeric-only with no v2 prefix structure

axes.name: vacuous-PASS mechanism — sha256 of empty string identity
axes.mechanism: serializer exits 2 → stdout empty → both Layer 4 invocations produce identical empty output → shasum -a 256 of empty = e3b0c44298fc1c14d29... → sha1==sha2 → byte-eq trivially holds → printf 'PASS' → __ATLAS_DSL_V2_REGRESSION__ PASS
axes.anti_pattern: byte-eq idempotence (raw 68) collapses into vacuous identity when both sides are empty; need orthogonal non-emptiness witness

axes.name: OPT selection — OPT-B preferred (regression script fix)
axes.OPT_A_extend_serializer_cost: extend grade parser to accept dual-markers + alien-index alphabet + bare numeric requires (i) loosening prefix marker validator, (ii) adding alien-index token family, (iii) deciding canonical emit order across heterogeneous grade languages — large semantic surface, risks raw 73 admissibility regression on existing v2 contract
axes.OPT_B_layer4_fix_cost: swap LAYER4_SHARD from atlas.n6 to atlas.append.nexus-historical-absorption-2026-04-26.n6 (95 v2-compat entries, exercises rich v3 compound grade allowlist) — single-file change, preserves Layer-4 stress intent (95 entries ≈ SAMPLE=100), zero serializer change
axes.decision: OPT-B chosen. Atlas main → v2 migration is a SEPARATE ω-cycle (grade language unification), not part of F23 falsifier resolution. F23 only requires Layer 4 to be a real test, not vacuous.

axes.name: implementation — Layer 4 rewrite with anti-vacuous hardening
changes: header preservation via awk '/^@[A-Z]/ {exit} {print}' (handles arbitrary comment-block depth, not hardcoded head -10)
captured serializer exit codes ec1/ec2 + stdout out1/out2 in variables → assert ec=0 AND non-empty before sha comparison
if exit≠0 → FAIL loudly with 'serializer non-zero exit (ec1=… ec2=…)'
if stdout empty → FAIL loudly with 'serializer empty stdout (vacuous-PASS guard tripped)'
F23-resolution comment block (lines 143-159) explains vacuous-PASS mechanism for future readers

before.evidence: bash tool/atlas_dsl_v2_regression.sh --layer 4 --sample 100 → 'Layer 4 [main subset N=100] PASS — byte-eq sha e3b0c44298fc1c14 (100 entries processed)'

after.evidence_layer4: Layer 4 [v2-compat subset N=100 shard=atlas.append.nexus-historical-absorption-2026-04-26.n6] PASS — byte-eq sha aaf8571e72559e87 (95 entries processed)
after.evidence_F23_falsifier: bash …/atlas_dsl_v2_regression.sh --layer 4 --sample 100 2>&1 | grep -q 'sha e3b0c44298fc1c14' && echo VACUOUS_PASS || echo NON_EMPTY  →  NON_EMPTY

axes.name: anti-vacuous guard reverse-witness
axes.result: Layer 4 [v2-compat subset] FAIL — serializer non-zero exit (ec1=2 ec2=2) shard=atlas.n6  →  exit 2  →  __ATLAS_DSL_V2_REGRESSION__ FAIL
axes.interpretation: if a future commit accidentally points Layer 4 back at atlas main (or any v2-incompat source), the regression script now FAILs LOUDLY with explicit ec=2 instead of vacuously passing — F23 mechanism permanently sealed at the script level, independent of falsifier coverage

axes.name: F23 falsifier disposition — keep CLEAN, do not retire
axes.decision: F23 stays in falsifiers.jsonl with status:CLEAN — guards against any future regression where Layer 4 sha would collapse back to e3b0c44298fc1c14… (e.g. someone re-introduces atlas-main subset, or breaks anti-vacuous guards). Per raw 71, falsifiers retire only when the underlying property they assert becomes structurally unreachable; here the property (Layer-4 non-vacuity) is exactly what we want continued protection on.
axes.patch_falsifiers_jsonl: F23 entry: claim updated to 'v2-compat shard subset', fix updated to RESOLVED 2026-04-26 (OPT-B), status:CLEAN, resolved_at:2026-04-26, resolution_witness pointer added

axes.name: follow-on observation — atlas-main grade-language migration is a separate ω-cycle
axes.observation: atlas.n6 (21850 lines) carries 52 distinct grade tokens spanning ≥4 grade-languages: v1-numeric, v1-marker, v1-dual-marker [10*!], alien-index alpha [A]…[XY]. v2 serializer admits one canonical sublanguage (numeric+single-marker+compound allowlist). Migration is high-value (enables atlas-main → JSON ingest, dashboards, cross-engine query) but out-of-scope for F23 — would warrant a dedicated v9/v10 spec ω-cycle covering: marker-set extension, alien-index taxonomy mapping, lossless numeric-only handling, and re-promotion of atlas main as Layer-4 source.
axes.deferred_for: future ω-cycle — atlas DSL v2 grade-language unification (v9 marker-set extension + v10 alien-index admit)

files_unchanged_by_design: tool/atlas_dsl_v2_serializer.hexa — no parser change; v3 compound-grade contract preserved per raw 73
